title: rumex bucephalophorus subsp. domesticus
intro: Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. domesticus, commonly known as the garden sorrel or common sorrel, is a perennial plant species within the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent. The health benefits associated with Rumex bucephalophorus subsp. domesticus include its potential to reduce blood pressure and improve digestive health, due to its high vitamin C and flavonoid content. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used as a culinary ingredient in some cuisines, adding a sour flavor to dishes.
